title: pycnanthemum verticillatum var. pilosum
intro: Pycnanthemum verticillatum var. pilosum, also known as Mountain Mint, is a perennial plant that belongs to the mint family (Lamiaceae) and is native to North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, respiratory issues, and skin conditions, offering benefits such as relief from nausea, coughs, and skin irritations. Mountain Mint can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and its leaves can also be used as a natural breath freshener and digestive aid.
